  "decision": "fail",

  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Water being added to concrete mix on site without approval, altering slump beyond mix design limits",
    "Excess water increases water-cement ratio, reducing compressive strength and durability of placed concrete",
    "Unapproved slump manipulation violates batch ticket and mix design requirements",
    "Concrete placed with altered mix will be installed-defective and non-compliant with structural specifications"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Immediately halt water addition to the concrete mix",
    "Reject and remove from placement any concrete that has had unapproved water added",
    "Verify slump with field technician using standard slump cone test per ASTM C143",
    "Obtain batch ticket and confirm water content against approved mix design before any further placement",
    "Notify QC manager and engineer of record for disposition of affected concrete",
    "Document the violation and retain samples for testing if any altered concrete has already been placed"
  ],
  "rationale": "Adding water on site to manipulate slump is a serious non-compliance that directly compromises the structural integrity of the concrete. ACI 301 and ACI 304 prohibit adding water beyond approved mix design limits without authorization. The increased water-cement ratio reduces compressive strength, durability, and may lead to long-term structural failure in a heavy-industrial setting where concrete performance is critical to safety.",
  "references": ["ACI 301", "ACI 304", "ASTM C143"]
}
